title: The Pottery Class
introKo:
도자기 공방 첫 수업, Clay 선생님이 물레를 손수 가르쳐 주는 아주 건전한 이야기.
…표면적으로는요. 단어를 탭하면 속뜻이 보여요.

quiz.q: Clay 선생님이 물레에서 가장 먼저 가르쳐 준 것은?

normal.text: Your first pottery class begins tonight. The studio is warm and smells of wet earth. Mrs. Clay welcomes you at the door.
"Tonight we work with our hands," she says. "Clay does not listen to words. It only listens to fingers."
She puts a ball of wet clay on the wheel. "Do not be afraid to get your hands wet," she says. "Clay loves warm hands."

options.en: I have never touched clay before.
options.reaction: "Then tonight will be special," she smiles. "Untouched clay learns the fastest."

options.en: My hands are ready. Where do we start?
options.reaction: "Right here," she says. "The wheel loves ready hands."

normal.text: The wheel begins to turn. The wet clay slips and slides under your fingers. It will not stay still.
"First, you must find the center," Mrs. Clay says. She steps behind you and puts her hands over yours. "Feel the rhythm. Do not fight it."

options.en: It keeps slipping away from me.
options.reaction: "Then hold it firmer, dear," she says softly. "Good hands are firm and gentle at the same time."
"Exactly," she says. "Slow and steady. You learn fast."

normal.text: Together, four hands press the clay, and it finds its center. "There," she says, stepping back. "When it is centered, it stops slipping and starts to dance."
"Now we pull it up. Wet your hands again — clay only rises when it is wet." Slowly, the clay rises between your fingers.
"Gently now," she says. "Squeeze softly and let it rise. See how tall it stands? It only rises like this for patient hands."

options.en: I did not know my hands could do this.
options.ko: 제 손이 이런 걸 할 수 있는 줄 몰랐어요.
options.reaction: "Every hand can," she says. "It only needs the right teacher. Lucky for you, I give private lessons."

options.en: Is it always this warm in here?
options.reaction: "That is the kiln, dear," she laughs. "Things get much hotter before they are finished."

normal.text: At the end of class, your little bowl goes into the kiln. "Now the fire finishes what our hands began," Mrs. Clay says. "The heat makes it strong."
You walk home in the cool night air. Your hands still remember the rhythm of the wheel. Next week, you will come back — for the pottery, of course.

hard.text: Your first pottery class begins tonight, in a studio that smells of wet earth and warm dust. Mrs. Clay greets you at the door with an easy smile.
"Tonight we let our hands do the talking," she announces. "Clay ignores words. It answers only to fingers."
She drops a ball of wet clay onto the wheel. "Don't be afraid of getting your hands wet," she says. "Clay has always preferred warm hands."

options.en: I have never touched clay before in my life.
options.ko: 저 점토 태어나서 처음 만져 봐요.
options.reaction: "Then tonight will be a special one," she smiles. "Untouched clay learns the fastest."

options.en: My hands are ready. Where do we start?
options.reaction: "Right here," she says. "The wheel has a weakness for ready hands."

hard.text: The wheel begins to spin, and the wet clay slips and slides under your fingers, refusing to stay where you want it.
"Before anything else, you must find the center," Mrs. Clay says, stepping behind you and folding her hands over yours. "Feel the rhythm of the wheel. Don't fight it — move with it."

options.en: It keeps slipping away from me.
options.reaction: "Then hold it a little firmer, dear," she murmurs. "The best hands are firm and gentle at the very same time."
"Exactly so," she says. "Slow and steady. You learn remarkably fast."

hard.text: Together, four hands press down, and the clay finally finds its center. "There," she says, stepping back. "Once it is centered, it stops slipping away and starts to dance."
"Now comes the pull. Wet your hands again — clay only rises when it is properly wet." Slowly, almost shyly, the clay rises between your fingers.
"Gently now," she says. "A soft squeeze, a patient lift, and let it rise. See how tall it stands? It rises like this only for hands that take their time."

options.en: I never knew my hands could do this.
options.ko: 제 손이 이런 걸 할 수 있는 줄 몰랐어요.
options.reaction: "Every hand can," she says. "It simply needs the right teacher. Lucky for you, I give private lessons."

options.en: Is it always this warm in here?
options.reaction: "That would be the kiln, dear," she laughs. "Things get considerably hotter before they are finished."

hard.text: At the end of class, your little bowl disappears into the kiln. "Now the fire finishes what our hands began," Mrs. Clay says. "Nothing becomes strong without a little heat."
You walk home through the cool night air, your hands still remembering the rhythm of the wheel. Next week you will be back — for the pottery, naturally.

firmer.ko: 더 단단히, 더 힘 있게 (firm의 비교급)

weakness.ko: 약점 (have a weakness for: ~에 약하다, ~을 아주 좋아하다)

wink.clay: 재료 이름이자 선생님 성함이에요. 'Clay는 따뜻한 손을 좋아해요' 같은 문장들, 재료 얘기로 한 번, 자기소개로 한 번 — 두 번 읽어 보세요.
wink.wet: 점토는 젖어 있어야 빚어져요. …이 말을 공방 밖에서 하면 분위기가 조금 달라질 수 있어요.
wink.rhythm: 물레의 리듬을 느끼라는 조언이지만, 둘이 호흡을 맞추는 장면에서는 묘하게 들리죠.
wink.firmer: '조금 더 단단히'는 순수한 물레 조언이에요. 등 뒤에서 나직이 말하면 얘기가 달라지지만요.
wink.rises: 점토가 손끝에서 천천히 올라오는 것뿐인데, 왠지 문장을 두 번 읽게 돼요.
wink.squeeze: 부드럽게 쥐라는 정식 도예 용어예요. 맥락이 맥락이라 귀가 먼저 반응할 뿐이에요.
wink.pull: '끌어올리기'는 실제 물레 기법 이름이에요. 최대한 진지한 표정으로 읽어 주세요.
wink.hotter: 가마는 원래 갈수록 뜨거워져요. 이 대화도 그런 것 같지만 기분 탓이에요.
wink.heat: 열기가 있어야 단단해진다는 건 어디까지나 도자기 얘기예요. 아마도요.

introEn:
Mrs. Clay teaches the pottery wheel, hands over hands. Perfectly wholesome.
...on the surface. Tap words to see the hidden meaning.
